summary: Bulk merge with no PR body or review discussion captured. The diff decompiled Ness yoyo item logic by replacing a MWERKS_GEKKO inline-asm-only implementation plus NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with C, and cleaned up grCastle_801CDFD8 by removing redundant locals while preserving the same random range and castle9 field initialization behavior.

key_files.role: Replaced the MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly and NOT_IMPLEMENTED fallback for it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk with C.
Simplified grCastle_801CDFD8 by collapsing redundant temporaries into direct parameter and field accesses.

what_changed: In src/melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c, it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk is now a C function instead of a MWERKS_GEKKO asm block guarded away from a NOT_IMPLEMENTED non-Gekko fallback.

The new it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk gets the Item with GET_ITEM(gobj), calls it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj), then performs a volatile load/compare through ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 against ref_gobj with the result discarded.
In src/melee/gr/grcastle.c, grCastle_801CDFD8 now uses a single local named range for grCs_804D6970->xA before HSD_Randi, instead of params/random_range scaffolding.

grCastle_801CDFD8 now assigns gp->gv.castle9.xD4 directly from grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result and writes -1 or 0 literals directly to xDC, xDA, xD8, and xD6, removing extra locals such as neg_one, zero, base_value, and final_value.
smart_moves: The Ness yoyo C replacement preserves the visible call order from the asm: obtain the item pointer, call it_8026B894, then read the owner/ref field afterward.
Using HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr plus a discarded ternary comparison is a targeted matching tactic for forcing the post-call load/compare without introducing gameplay-visible logic.

The grCastle cleanup removes decompiler-style one-use temporaries and keeps the zero-range fallback for HSD_Randi explicit, making the code easier to review without changing the apparent control flow.
Removing the MWERKS_GEKKO/#else split makes it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk searchable and maintainable as normal C instead of hiding the only implementation in inline assembly.
decomp_lessons: When original assembly performs a load and compare whose result is unused, a volatile pointer access with a discarded expression can be useful to preserve emitted instructions while keeping the C behavior inert.
For matched stage initialization code, redundant locals introduced during decompilation can often be collapsed into direct struct field accesses once the compiler output is understood.
Field type assignments can sometimes replace explicit cast temporaries, as seen with gp->gv.castle9.xD4 receiving grCs_804D6970->x8 + rand_result directly instead of using a separate s16 final_value.
Bulk PRs with no body/comments should be indexed from concrete diff evidence only; this slice does not identify the individual source PRs despite the title.
naming_conventions: Existing unknown/mapped function names were retained: grCastle_801CDFD8 and it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk.
Subsystem prefixes remained consistent: grCastle_/grCs_ for castle stage code and it_/Item_ for item code.
The grCastle local random_range was shortened to range, matching its role as the argument range for HSD_Randi.
The item implementation uses the common local ip for Item* from GET_ITEM(gobj).
Offset-style field names remained in place for not-yet-fully-named data, including xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and castle9 fields xD4, xD6, xD8, xDA, xDC, and xDE_b0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the inline asm implementation of it_2725_Logic59_EvtUnk containing nofralloc, stack frame setup, r31/r30 saves, a call to it_8026B894, a load from offset 0xDE4, and cmplw against ref_gobj.
Recreated the asm's post-call load/compare in C with HSD_GObj* volatile* owner_ptr = &ip->xDD4_itemVar.nessyoyo.x10 and (void) ((*owner_ptr == ref_gobj) ? 0 : 0).
Kept the item pointer acquisition before it_8026B894, matching the asm pattern where the item was loaded before the call and dereferenced after the call.
In grCastle_801CDFD8, direct literal assignments of -1 and 0 replaced named constant locals, a cleanup tactic when those locals are not needed for matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the captured PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on the two-file diff only.
